supersedes.reason: Phase A proposed 'mirror exact bytes' for 7 assets under LlamaIndex's downstream Apache-2.0 declaration. Phase B1 corrects that recommendation: a downstream dataset licence does not by itself grant redistribution rights over embedded third-party documents. Every entry here is redistribution=reference-fetch-only. Per-asset reclassification to 'direct' requires independent evidence of the underlying document's copyright, licence, and the owner's explicit permission.

notes: This is the FROZEN reference-fetch lockfile. Every entry pins the canonical HuggingFace source path and its expected label / defect_kind carried forward from the Phase A audit; no binary was downloaded, no checksum was captured, no mirror URL was invented.

Every asset entry has redistribution='reference-fetch-only'. Do NOT change this to 'direct' without an entry in the rights_review_queue below that documents the underlying document's copyright + explicit owner permission.

mirror_url, sha256, and size_bytes remain null. They will only be populated by an authorised fetch step in a later PR, and only for assets that stay reference-fetch-only (checksum-of-fetched-file, not checksum-for-mirror).
Do not treat public availability on HuggingFace as redistribution permission. LlamaIndex offers reference-fetch access; that is not equivalent to a per-file rights grant.

dataset_source.dataset_revision_verification: All 12 asset paths listed in this lockfile were confirmed to exist at this exact SHA via the HuggingFace tree/blob metadata endpoints. Paths were verified: NO PDF BODY WAS FETCHED at this phase.
dataset_source.dataset_revision_note: This SHA is the pinned reference target for the fetcher. Do not swap for a mutable branch like 'main'.
dataset_source.copyright_statement: All documents are sourced from public online channels. The dataset is released under the Apache 2.0 License. If there are any copyright concerns, please contact us via the GitHub repository. (Source: ParseBench README on HuggingFace, checked 2026-07-18.)
attribution_requirements: Retain the Apache-2.0 licence notice + text (or a link to the licence) in any redistribution.
Cite Zhang et al., 2026 (arXiv:2604.08538) alongside any use of the dataset.
State clearly that source documents originate from ParseBench, not from the AksharaMD project.
dataset_source.underlying_document_caveat: ParseBench asserts the dataset compilation is Apache-2.0 and offers a takedown pathway rather than a per-file rights registry. Individual documents may still be under upstream publisher, corporate, or third-party copyright. This lockfile assumes exactly that: reference-fetch-only for every entry unless the rights_review_queue records affirmative permission.

assets.license_caveat: Applies to the dataset compilation. Underlying document's original copyright not verified.
assets.copyright_owner: LlamaIndex (compilation); original document from public online channel, exact provenance not stated per-file
assets.attribution: Cite ParseBench (Zhang et al., 2026, arXiv:2604.08538) + retain Apache-2.0 notice.

pages.evidence: Extracted line "AANLCP JOURN A L … • Continual changes have been relationship, and look forward made to the website which is chock to hearing their ideas" splices content from 3 columns into a single reading-order sequence. Block-level W_MULTICOLUMN_ORDER also fires.

assets.notes: Historical primary TP that drove threshold=0.28 calibration (validators/multicolumn.py:133).

assets.license_caveat: Applies to the compilation. Filename indicates IKEA-branded marketing content — original copyright likely IKEA. Independent permission not verified.
assets.copyright_owner: LlamaIndex (compilation); underlying appears IKEA (Inter IKEA Systems B.V.).

pages.evidence: Magazine catalogue spread with multiple content zones (headings, thumbnails, prices). Extraction produces 51 short blocks and 7 HEADING_SKIP warnings. The problem class is layout complexity, not multi-column reading order per se.

assets.notes: Known SPAN-LEVEL FN class (validators/multicolumn.py:163). Block-level detector cannot see the damage by design.

assets.license_caveat: Applies to the compilation. Filename indicates a page from El País (Spanish newspaper) — publisher copyright applies.
assets.copyright_owner: LlamaIndex (compilation); underlying likely Prisa / El País.

pages.evidence: Spanish-newspaper front page (El Pais) with 4-column body plus banner headline, subhead, image caption, and boxed sidebars. Extraction reports OK 79 with 4 HEADING warnings but no W_MULTICOLUMN_ORDER — a span-level splice class the block-level detector does not observe.

pages.evidence: Clean two-column academic page. Extraction yields 8 blocks band HIGH 85 with only HEADING_HIERARCHY. Block sequence appears column-first-sorted; span-level splices possible but not verified from block output alone.

assets.notes: Known SPAN-LEVEL FN class. Sibling filenames (simple2col, simple2col2) exist — treat simple2 as the exact primary.

pages.evidence: Japanese magazine-style page (large photo + right-hand vertical Japanese text + multiple columns of body text). Extraction 1 block POOR 47 OCR_REQUIRED. Japanese script not decoded; multicolumn evaluation not applicable.

assets.notes: Identity resolved from historical evidence, NOT by preference. Cited exactly as text_dense__japanese in benchmarks/READINESS_CALIBRATION_DEV_REPORT.md line 280 (rs=85 band=HIGH signals=3/3 fail), line 303 ('text_dense__japanese: The readiness score sees a cleanly-structured PDF...'), line 449 ('Image-only / OCR unavailable (letter3, myctophidae, japanese)'), and line 473 ('japanese: 85→55'). The 5 candidate multilang/multicolumns Japanese files were NOT the historical fixture.

promotion_history.source_artefact_note: sha256 and size_bytes copied from the reviewed checksum artefact merged as PR #62 (squashed as 086c885); values independently recomputed from the local cache at review time.

corrections.reason: Historical label put this in block-level TP class, but the extraction shows mid-line splicing across columns — span-level. Block-level detector still fires (W_MULTICOLUMN_ORDER present).

Historical label said span-level FN, but visually this is an IKEA catalogue magazine spread with a MIXED layout of headings, thumbnails, prices, captions — not a classic column-interleaving case. Multicolumn detector applicability is limited.
This is an IMAGE-only PDF requiring OCR. Extraction returns 1 block POOR 47 with OCR_REQUIRED. Not a multicolumn asset at all — the historical block-level detector cannot apply.
IMAGE-only PDF requiring OCR. Not a multicolumn asset.
The historical Japanese fixture (text_dense__japanese) is an image / non-decoded page — extraction fires OCR_REQUIRED and produces 1 block POOR 47. The multicolumn detector cannot apply.

page_calibration_summary.schema_note: These counts are computed from per-asset fields in this same lockfile. See tests/test_parsebench_page_ground_truth.py::test_page_calibration_summary_parity for the derivation rule.

notes: Only assets with review_status=complete and no page marked extraction_status=ambiguous are counted.
Ambiguous assets and non-multicolumn assets are EXCLUDED from these counts.
This corpus is what the expanded page-level recalibration must run against.
